sourceTitle: YouTube Marketing ROI: Formula, Real Case Study Data + Free Calculator
postAngle: YouTube ROI is calculable but most B2B teams guess instead of measuring. Real case study data shows 3.25x conversion advantage over blogging.
linkedinPost:
"What's the ROI of YouTube?"

Every B2B founder asks this before committing budget. And almost every answer they get is vague hand-waving about brand awareness.

So we ran the numbers across a 12-month case study. Compared YouTube against blogging for the same business, same offer, same audience.

The result: YouTube drove 3.25x more conversions. Not views. Conversions.

The difference comes down to one thing teams ignore: intent density per viewer. A 2,000-view video targeting someone actively researching your service will outperform a 50,000-view blog post built for general interest.

But here's what surprised us: the ROI gap widened every quarter. Month 1 looked similar across both channels. By month 12, YouTube was pulling away because old videos kept ranking and converting while old blog posts flatlined.

There's a simple formula behind this. Four inputs, one number that tells you whether YouTube is worth it for your specific business.

We published the full formula, the case study data, and a free calculator so you can plug in your own numbers.

https://sellontube.com/blog/youtube-marketing-roi

#YouTubeROI #B2BMarketing
